title: सूर्य कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सूर्य कवचम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री सूर्यकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य याज्ञवल्क्य ऋषिः।
त्रिष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री सूर्यनारायणो देवता।
ह्रां बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। ह्रूं कीलकम्।
श्री सूर्यनारायणप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
ध्यायेत्सूर्यमनन्तकोटिकिरणं तेजोमयं भास्करं
भक्तानामभयप्रदं दिनकरं ज्योतिर्मयं शंकरम्।
आदित्यं सकलागमैकवरदं चैकं जगत्साक्षिणं
पद्मासीनमहं नमामि सततं भास्वन्तमार्तार्तिहम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥

घृणिः पातु शिरो देशं सूर्यः पातु ललाटकम्।
आदित्यो लोचने पातु श्रुती पातु प्रभाकरः॥१॥

घ्राणं पातु सदा भानुर्मुखं मे पातु भास्करः।
जिह्वां पातु जगन्नाथः कण्ठं पातु विभावसुः॥२॥

स्कन्धौ प्रभाकरः पातु भुजौ पातु दिवाकरः।
करौ धर्मकरः पातु हृदयं पातु भास्करः॥३॥

मध्यं पातु सुषुम्नेशो नाभिं मे पातु भास्करः।
कटिं पातु सहस्रांशुरुरू मे पातु भास्करः॥४॥

जानुनी पातु मार्तण्डो जंघे मे पातु भास्करः।
पादौ द्वादशनामा मे गुल्फौ पातु विभावसुः॥५॥

सर्वाण्यङ्गानि मे नित्यं पातु संसारतारकः।
सूर्यः पातु सदा प्राणान् सवितापातु मे बलम्॥६॥

पूर्वे पातु सहस्रांशुराग्नेय्यां पातु भास्करः।
दक्षिणे पातु वरदो नैरृत्यां पातु खेचरः॥७॥

पश्चिमे पातु सर्वात्मा वायव्यां पातु दीपकः।
उदीच्यां पातु सुप्रभो ऐशान्यां पातु कश्यपः॥८॥

ऊर्ध्वं पातु सहस्रार्चिरधस्ताद्विश्वतापनः।
प्रतिष्ठायां सदा पातु सन्ध्यायां च प्रकाशकः॥९॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
सूर्यकवचमिदं दिव्यं पठनीयं सदा नृभिः।
सर्वरोगहरं चैव सर्वपापप्रणाशनम्॥

सर्वशत्रुक्षयकरं सर्वमंगलदायकम्।
सर्वग्रहप्रशमनमायुरारोग्यवर्धनम्॥

प्रातःसूर्योदये पाठात्सर्वव्याधिविनाशनम्।
त्रिकालं यः पठेन्नित्यमजेयो भवति ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री भविष्यपुराणोक्तं श्री सूर्य कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Surya Kavacham ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Surya-kavach-stotra-mantrasya Yajnavalkya Rishiph.
Trishtup Chhandah. Shri Surya-Narayano Devata.
Hram Bijam. Hrim Shaktih. Hrum Kilakam.
Shri Surya-Narayana-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Dhyayet-Suryam-ananta-koti-kiranam Tejo-mayam Bhaskaram
Bhaktanam-abhaya-pradam Dinakaram Jyotir-mayam Shankaram.
Adityam Sakala-agama-eka-varadam Cha-ekam Jagat-sakshinam
Padma-asinam-aham Namami Satatam Bhasvantam-arta-artihm.

|| Kavacham ||

Ghrinih Patu Shiro Desham Suryah Patu Lalatakam.
Adityao Lochane Patu Shruti Patu Prabhakarah.||1||

Ghranam Patu Sada Bhanur-mukham Me Patu Bhaskarah.
Jihvam Patu Jagan-nathah Kantham Patu Vibhavasuh.||2||

Skandhau Prabhakarah Patu Bhujau Patu Divakarah.
Karau Dharma-karah Patu Hridayam Patu Bhaskarah.||3||

Madhyam Patu Sushumnesho Nabhim Me Patu Bhaskarah.
Katim Patu Sahasramshu-ruru Me Patu Bhaskarah.||4||

Januni Patu Martando Janghe Me Patu Bhaskarah.
Padau Dvadasha-nama Me Gulphau Patu Vibhavasuh.||5||

Sarvany-angani Me Nityam Patu Samsara-tarakah.
Suryah Patu Sada Pranan Savita-apatu Me Balam.||6||

Purve Patu Sahasramshu-ragneyam Patu Bhaskarah.
Dakshine Patu Varado Nairityam Patu Khecharah.||7||

Pashchime Patu Sarvatma Vayavyam Patu Dipakah.
Udichyam Patu Suprabho Aishanyam Patu Kashyapah.||8||

Urdhvam Patu Sahasrarchir-adhastad-vishva-tapanah.
Pratishthayam Sada Patu Sandhyayam cha Prakashakah.||9||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Surya-kavacham-idam Divyam Pathaniyam Sada Nribhih.
Sarva-roga-haram Chaiva Sarva-papa-pranashanam.

Sarva-shatru-kshaya-karam Sarva-mangala-dayakam.
Sarva-graha-prashamanam Ayur-arogya-vardhanam.

Pratah-Surya-udaye Pathat-sarva-vyadhi-vinashanam.
Tri-kalam Yah Pathen-nityam Ajeyo Bhavati Dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Bhavishya-puranoktam Shri Surya Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान — अनन्तकोटि किरणों वाले, तेजोमय, भक्तों को अभय देने वाले, दिनकर, ज्योतिर्मय, शंकर (कल्याणकारी), सभी आगमों में एकमात्र वरद, जगत के साक्षी, पद्मासीन, पीड़ितों की पीड़ा हरने वाले सूर्य को मैं सदा नमस्कार करता हूँ।

शिर की 'घृणि' (सूर्यरश्मि), ललाट की सूर्य, नेत्रों की आदित्य, कानों की प्रभाकर रक्षा करें। नासिका की भानु, मुख की भास्कर, जिह्वा की जगन्नाथ, कण्ठ की विभावसु रक्षा करें।

कन्धों की प्रभाकर, भुजाओं की दिवाकर, हस्तों की धर्मकर, हृदय की भास्कर, मध्य भाग की सुषुम्नेश, नाभि की भास्कर, कटि की सहस्रांशु रक्षा करें।

पूर्व में सहस्रांशु, दक्षिण में वरद, पश्चिम में सर्वात्मा, उत्तर में सुप्रभ, ऊपर सहस्रार्चि, नीचे विश्वतापन रक्षा करें।

प्रातः सूर्योदय के समय पाठ करने से सभी व्याधियाँ नष्ट होती हैं। त्रिकाल पाठ करने वाला अजेय हो जाता है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीसूर्यकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य याज्ञवल्क्य ऋषिः। त्रिष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीसूर्यनारायणो देवता। ह्रां बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। ह्रूं कीलकम्। श्रीसूर्यनारायणप्रीत्यर्थे सूर्यकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides complete divine armor protection of Lord Surya (Sun God)
Associated in tradition with all diseases especially eye ailments and skin conditions
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and bestows auspiciousness in all endeavors
Pacifies all planetary afflictions, especially Surya-related doshas
Three-times daily recitation makes the devotee invincible
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Sun God Surya, Surya Kavacham (सूर्य कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Attributed to Bhavishya Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all sins and bestow auspiciousness in all endeavors and provide complete divine armor protection of Lord Surya (Sun God). It is also said to cure all diseases especially eye ailments and skin conditions. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti and Ratha Saptami. The text contains 9 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Surya Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Surya Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Kavacham (सूर्य कवचम्) is a powerful Sanskrit divine-armor (kavacha) stotra invoking the protective powers of Lord Surya (the Sun God / Surya Narayana) over every part of the devotee's body. The text is from the Bhavishya Purana with the formal viniyoga attributing the rishi to Yajnavalkya, the chhanda to Trishtup, the devata to Sri Surya Narayana, the bija to Hram, the shakti to Hrim and the kilaka to Hrum. The stotra opens with a dhyana of Surya as ananta-koti-kirana (with infinite crores of rays), tejomaya, padma-asina (lotus-seated), and proceeds with the body-protection sequence (anga-nyasa kavacha): different solar names (Ghrini, Surya, Aditya, Prabhakara, Bhanu, Bhaskara, Jagan-natha, Vibhavasu, Divakara, Sahasramshu, Martanda, Savita, Khechara, Sushumnesha, Sahasrarchi, Vishva-tapana etc.) protect different limbs (head, forehead, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, tongue, neck, shoulders, arms, hands, heart, navel, hips, thighs, knees, calves, feet) and the eight cardinal directions. (Source: Bhavishya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Surya and why is he worshipped as the Sun God?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Surya (Surya Narayana) is the visible deity (pratyaksha-devata) of Hinduism — the only Hindu god who can be directly seen in the sky every day. He is one of the twelve Adityas (sons of Aditi and Kashyapa Prajapati), with Vivasvan being his canonical Aditya-name; husband of Sanjna and Chhaya; father of Vaivasvata Manu (the progenitor of the present human race), Yama (Lord of Death), Yamuna, Ashvini Kumaras, Karna and Sugriva. He rides the seven-horse chariot (sapta-ashva-ratha) driven by the dawn-charioteer Aruna across the sky, has 1000 names (Surya Sahasranama), and is the source of all light, heat, life, time and consciousness in the universe. He is the central deity of the Saura sect (one of the six pancha-yatana sects established by Adi Shankaracharya — Saura, Shaiva, Vaishnava, Shakta, Ganapatya, Kaumara), and his greatest temples include Konark (Odisha), Modhera (Gujarat), Martand (Kashmir), Surya Pahar (Assam) and the Surya Pranita Devalaya at Kashi. (Sources: Bhavishya Purana; Markandeya Purana; Aditya Hridayam.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the body-protection (anga-nyasa) structure of the Surya Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Kavacham follows the classical kavacha-stotra structure of complete body protection through anga-nyasa — the systematic placement of solar names on each body part. The protection sequence is: (1) Ghrini protects the head (shiras), Surya the forehead (lalata), Aditya the eyes (lochane), Prabhakara the ears (shruti); (2) Bhanu protects the nose (ghrana), Bhaskara the mouth (mukha), Jagan-natha the tongue (jihva), Vibhavasu the neck (kantha); (3) Prabhakara the shoulders (skandhau), Divakara the arms (bhujau), Dharma-kara the hands (karau), Bhaskara the heart (hridaya); (4) Sushumnesha the middle (madhya), Bhaskara the navel (nabhi), Sahasramshu the hips (kati) and thighs (uru); (5) Martanda the knees (januni), Bhaskara the calves (janghe), Dvadasha-nama the feet (padau), Vibhavasu the ankles (gulphau). Then directional protection: Sahasramshu in the East, Bhaskara in South-East, Varada in South, Khechara in South-West, Sarvatma in West, Dipaka in North-West, Suprabha in North, Kashyapa in North-East, Sahasrarchi above, Vishva-tapana below. (Source: Surya Kavacham; Bhavishya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Surya Kavacham be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Kavacham is most powerfully recited on Ratha Saptami (Magha Shukla Saptami — Surya Jayanti, the supreme annual birthday of Surya), Makar Sankranti (Surya's transit into Capricorn marking Uttarayana), Chhath Puja (the four-day Surya festival of Bihar/UP/Jharkhand), Samba Dashami, all Sankranti days (the 12 monthly Surya transits), Sunday (Ravivar — the weekly day of Surya), and during a Surya Mahadasha or Antardasha in vimshottari dasha. The phala-shruti specifies pratah-Surya-udaye pathat — recitation at sunrise destroys all diseases — and trikalam yah pathen-nityam — three-times daily recitation makes the devotee invincible (ajeyo bhavati dhruvam). The traditional vidhi prescribes facing east (Surya-direction), seated on a kusha-asana, having performed Arghya offering of water from a copper vessel containing red flowers, vermillion (kumkum) and akshata. The kavacha is also recited before any solar yajna, Surya Namaskar practice, or Aditya-puja. (Sources: Drikpanchang Surya festivals; Surya upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Surya Kavacham grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti of the Surya Kavacham promises remarkable blessings: (1) Sarva-roga-haram — destruction of all diseases, especially those ruled by the Sun in jyotisha (eye disorders, vision problems, skin diseases, heart conditions, bone weakness, diabetes, hair loss, baldness, headaches, low immunity); (2) Sarva-papa-pranashanam — destruction of all sins; (3) Sarva-shatru-kshaya-karam — destruction of all enemies and conspiracies; (4) Sarva-mangala-dayakam — bestowal of all forms of auspiciousness; (5) Sarva-graha-prashamanam — pacification of all 9 grahas (planetary doshas), especially Surya graha-dosha (low Sun in horoscope, Sun-Saturn opposition, Sun-Rahu/Ketu eclipse, Pitru dosha caused by debilitated Surya); (6) Ayur-arogya-vardhanam — increase of longevity, vitality and ojas; (7) Pratah-pathat sarva-vyadhi-vinashanam — sunrise recitation destroys all bodily ailments; (8) Trikalam pathana — three-times daily recitation makes the devotee ajeya (invincible). It is also blessing for father, governmental authority, leadership, reputation and royal status — all Surya karakas. (Source: Surya Kavacham phala-shruti.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Surya Kavacham, Aditya Hridayam and Surya Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Three major Surya stotras exist with overlapping but distinct functions. (1) Aditya Hridayam — a 31-verse hymn from the Yuddha Kanda of the Valmiki Ramayana (sarga 105), taught to Sri Rama by sage Agastya on the battlefield before the slaying of Ravana — is the supreme stotra-prarthana (devotional praise) of Surya, focusing on his cosmic glory and victory-giving power. It is a stuti, not a kavacha. (2) Surya Kavacham — the present text from the Bhavishya Purana with rishi Yajnavalkya — is a kavacha (armor-stotra) with anga-nyasa structure, focusing on body-protection and disease-removal through the placement of solar names on each limb. (3) Surya Ashtakam — an eight-verse stotra (also called Aditya-ashtakam) — is a shorter daily devotional in praise of Surya. Devotees combine all three — recite the Surya Kavacham for protection, the Aditya Hridayam for victory and the Surya Ashtakam for daily devotion. The Surya Kavacham is most specifically prescribed for graha-dosha pacification and disease removal. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana 6.105; Bhavishya Purana.)
